content.full_text:
MICHAEL B. BTXLER
1192 Seacoast Drive
Imperial Beach, CA 91932
March 20, 1992
Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Department
428 "J" Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA 95814
Dear Sirs:
An issue has been raised as to a potential conflict of interest I may have with regard to an
upcoming vote on temporary zoning code amendments proposed to be enacted pursuant to
a litigation settlement agreement. I would like to request formal written advice pursuant to
Government Code section 83114 (b) from the Fair Political Practices Commission.
On April 17, 1991, a citizens' group filed a lawsuit against the City of Imperial Beach
alleging various deficiencies of the General Plan, including the fact that the City had not
revised its Housing Element since 1980. At the time the action was filed, the City was in
the process of revising the element, but it was not quite finished. After lengthy settlement
negotiations, a settlement agreement was entered into by the City Council on August 9,
1991. The terms of the settlement agreement provided that the City would either enact
temporary zoning amendments which would affect City's multi-unit zones, or a judgment
halting all development could be entered against the City until such time as a comprehensive
general plan amendment was enacted. Attached is a chart showing the changes that would
take place if the zoning amendments were passed.
My home is located on a double lot in the RHD zone and consists of one home in which
my family and I live (11924 Seacoast Drive), and another home which is rented (11928
Seacoast Drive). The iotal lot area of the two lots is approximately 12,000 sq. !t. I estimate
the market value of the lots, with improvements, as $1,250,000. In addition, I am the sole
beneficiary of a trust that is the record owner of a duplex which is also located in the RHD
zone and is located on 206 Elder/951 Second Street. The lot area is approximately 3,200 sq.
ft. and I estimate the fair market value as $250,000.
l..J
C,J
F€r
=
t-a,


FPPC
MNch 20, 1992
Page Two
The City of Imperial Beach has a population of 26,645 people, according to 1990 census
figures. There are approximately 285 acres of RHD zoned property in the City. The total
area of the City 2,863 acres, of which 246 acres is a military airfield and 902 acres is an open
spaceArildlife preserve. Thus the RHD district constitutes 16.6/o of the City, if the military
airfield and the preserve are not considered. The 1990 census reported that there were 4055
units that were part of developments containing three or more units, which, with a few
exceptions, are all located in either the RHD or R-2A Znnes. There are 7ll property
owners within the RHD district.
This issue was to be heard and decided on March L8, 1992. It was continued to April 1,
1992, so that any conflict of interest issues could be determined. As I understand it, time
is of the essence because the court could be persuaded to enter judgment against the City
of Imperial Beach should it not enact the zoning code amendments soon. It is also my
understanding that in order for the amendments to become final, they must be reviewed by
the Coastal Commission, which, unless there is further delay, is scheduled for May, 1992.
I request that you respond as quickly as possible. Thank you for your consideration.
Sincerely,
fu,,oL^rtrS l;fu
Michael B. Bixler
Mayor, City of Imperial Beach
MBB:lb

In each of the above zones, the following limitation on lot consolidations is also proposed:
No lots which are subject to this Chapter and which are combined or
consolidated into one lot pursuant to the Subdivision Map Act or otherwise
shall be allowed to leld a greater density of residential units than such lots
would have yielded prior to the combining of said lots. This provision shall
not apply when the resulting lot yields two or fewer residential units. This
provision is void after January I, 1993.
All amendments would sunset on January l, 1,993.
3
J

sections.question_synthetic: Does Michael B. Bixler, Mayor of Imperial Beach, have a conflict of interest in voting on proposed temporary zoning code amendments that would reduce density requirements in the RHD zone, where he owns residential property worth approximately $1.5 million?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a requestor (Mayor Bixler) to the FPPC seeking advice, not an FPPC response letter. The document contains detailed factual background about property holdings and zoning changes but no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. No FPPC response is included in this document.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Does Michael B. Bixler, Mayor of Imperial Beach, have a conflict of interest in voting on proposed temporary zoning code amendments that would reduce density requirements in the RHD zone, where he owns residential property worth approximately $1.5 million?

embedding.summary: Michael B. Bixler, Mayor of Imperial Beach, requests formal written FPPC advice regarding a potential conflict of interest in voting on temporary zoning amendments required by a litigation settlement agreement. Bixler owns property in the affected RHD zone and seeks guidance on whether he can vote on these amendments.
